summary: Excellent soundboard transfer with a mellow, well‑played show highlighted by a stellar Cryptical/Other One suite and an electric Ripple.
review: The recording is a high‑quality Betty Cantor soundboard transfer that sounds lively despite a few brief dropouts. Musically the concert is solid – the first set shines with tight jams, a standout Cryptical/Other One suite and superb performances of Me and My Uncle, Hard to Handle, Bird Song, Wharf Rat and an electrified Ripple; the second set is enjoyable but not as strong, with Loser’s vocals falling short.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviewers consistently praise the performance and note many standout songs while acknowledging only minor recording glitches; weighting favors the strong show quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, lively mix from a great source; a few annoying dropouts are mentioned but overall the transfer is praised as brilliant.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is tight and jam‑rich, especially the Cryptical/Other One suite; second set is decent but less cohesive, with a beautiful Ripple as its high point.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Jangly leads praised throughout (Me and My Uncle, Bird Song) and his tone described as “Duane Allman‑like”; vocal repeats noted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Laser‑focused attack on the Other One intro and a brooding movement later in the suite are highlighted.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s jangly rhythm guitar praised on Wharf Rat and Hard to Handle; strong lead work noted second set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s eerie organ fills on Loser and his rap on Lovelight receive special mention.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann adapts well after Mickey’s departure, delivering a solid drum break before the Other One and overall tight drumming.
